title: Mace Windu's quarters

The '''living quarters''' of Senior [[Jedi High Council/Legends|High Council]] member and former [[Master of the Order/Legends|Master of the Order]] [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] served him from his appointment to the High Council until the dissolution of the Order during the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]].

==History==
These quarters were assigned to Master Windu upon his appointment to the High Council. Within these chambers, Windu would [[Meditation/Legends|meditate]] on [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] and mentor any Jedi requesting counsel.<ref name="AOTC" />

Following the [[Mission to Er'stacia]], Master Windu met with Yoda in his chambers to review the mission.<ref name="Puzzle Peace">{{Tales|13|Puzzle Peace}}</ref> Later while meditating, the pair received a report from Knight [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] who was investigating the attempted [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]] of [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] on [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]. While on the water-world, Kenobi had met with [[Prime Minister/Legends|Prime Minister]] [[Lama Su/Legends|Lama Su]] who revealed the creation of the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. The Masters were shocked that Prime Minister Su claimed that the Jedi Council had placed the order and were even more concerned with the fact they had not sensed its creation through the Force. Realizing how clouded the Force was, the two decided to keep their diminishing abilities private so as not to boost the moral of the resurgent and mysterious [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]].<ref name="AOTC" />

Soon after the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]] and the outbreak of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Windu met with Master [[Tholme/Legends|Tholme]] and Jedi [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]] regarding a proposed undercover mission to get into the upper echelons of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. Vos wished to use his extensive spy network to get close to [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] and discover the identity of the [[Palpatine/Legends|second Sith]]. Windu said that he would consent, yet he would have to consult Master Yoda before Vos could depart. When Yoda approved, Vos left the Temple and began his war long campaign to undermine the Separatists.<ref name="Jedi: Count Dooku">''[[Jedi: Count Dooku]]''</ref>

While Jedi Kenobi was on a secret [[Mission to Zigoola|mission]] on [[Zigoola]], Master Yoda received a cry through the Force from his wayward Jedi. Early in the morning while the rest of the Temple slept, Yoda rushed to Windu's room and roused him from his sleep. Sitting up on his cot, Windu agreed that Yoda should send non-Jedi assistance so as to avoid the thrall of the Sith presence on the planet.<ref name="Clone Wars Gambit: Stealth">''[[Clone Wars Gambit: Stealth]]''</ref>

Following the [[Battle of Xagobah (Clone Wars)|Battle of Xagobah]], the young [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] encountered Jedi [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and revealed he had dire news for the [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]]. Skywalker arranged for [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]] to escort the bounty hunter to the Jedi Temple until an arrangement with the [[Office of the Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Office of the Supreme Chancellor]] could be made. Once there, Fett began to plot the assassination of Master Windu. In the time before Windu was due at the [[Office of the Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Chancellor's Office]] Fett sneaked through the service corridors of the Temple up to the Council Members' Level. Preparing to execute the Master, Fett opened Windu's door to find the room empty. Concluding that Windu had just left, the bounty hunter leapt out of the window and used his [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpack]] to fly to the [[Senate Building/Legends|Senate Building]].<ref name="Boba Fett: Pursuit">''[[Boba Fett: Pursuit]]''</ref>

Windu was stationed at the Temple when Supreme Chancellor [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] was discovered to be the elusive Darth Sidious. One of the only individuals in the galaxy to discover this terrible truth, Windu went to the Chancellor's Office and attempted to [[Showdown on Coruscant|arrest]] the Sith Lord. Falling victim to the Sith, Windu died and the Temple was seized by the Grand Army's [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] under [[Operation: Knightfall/Legends|Operation: Knightfall]].<ref name="ROTS" />

==Layout==
Located on the Council Members' Level of the [[High Council Tower/Legends|High Council Tower]], the room was accessed through a black door off a hallway that ringed the tower's central shaft. Bare and hexagonal, the room was empty save a few meditation pads and a sleep couch. The center of the room was occupied with a slim [[Holoprojector/Legends|holoprojector]] from which Windu could receive reports from Jedi in the field. Styled in a rusty-red hue, the chamber ringed by pilars which framed windows looking out onto the cityscape of [[Galactic City/Legends|Galactic City]].<ref name="AOTC" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Ep2-1a-60911.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Mace Windu at his desk.]]

Mace Windu's chamber in the Jedi Temple was originally going to be an executive office, with a desk behind which Mace would receive Obi-Wan Kenobi's report. However, [[George Lucas]] felt that the Jedi would not keep office space in the Temple, and Mace's chamber adopted the [[Wikipedia:Sparta|Spartan]] style of [[Yoda's quarters/Legends|Yoda's quarters]].

In the shooting script, the scene in Mace's quarters is listed as taking place in [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda's]] chamber.
